title: OIL SEAL REPLACEMENT

figures_detected.description: Installing oil seal using a suitable tool (camshaft or similar seal area)
Removing crankshaft front oil seal with a suitable tool after removing timing belt and sprocket
figures_detected.associated_step: Crankshaft front oil seal removal step 2

figures_detected.description: Installing crankshaft front oil seal using a suitable tool
figures_detected.associated_step: Crankshaft front oil seal installation step 3

figures_detected.description: Removing crankshaft rear oil seal with a suitable tool
figures_detected.associated_step: Crankshaft rear oil seal removal step 3

figures_detected.description: Installing crankshaft rear oil seal using a suitable tool
figures_detected.associated_step: Crankshaft rear oil seal installation step 4

steps.text: Apply engine oil to new oil seal and install oil seal using a suitable tool.
Remove timing belt, crankshaft sprocket and spacer.
Remove oil seal with a suitable tool.
Apply engine oil to new oil seal and install oil seal using a suitable tool.
Remove transaxle assembly. (Refer to "REMOVAL AND INSTALLATION" in MT section.)
Remove rear oil seal with a suitable tool.
Apply engine oil to new oil seal and install oil seal using a suitable tool.

tools_referenced.name: Suitable tool
tools_referenced.usage: Removing and installing camshaft, crankshaft front and rear oil seals

cross_references: MT section — REMOVAL AND INSTALLATION (transaxle assembly)
model_notes: Section code VJZ indicated in top-right corner. Crankshaft rear oil seal procedure references MT (Manual Transaxle) section.
